body:
Hi Roberto,

Following up on the ticket I submitted last week — still having the same issue with my route not logging correctly. My morning loop around Bushnell Park runs anywhere from 2 to 2.5 miles depending on which entrance I use, and the app keeps topping out at 1.8 no matter what I do. I'm out there every morning, dark roast already handled before I leave the house, so this is a daily frustration at this point.

I tried the recalibration steps the last agent walked me through and they didn't hold. Attaching another screenshot from this morning's session in case it helps your team see what's happening on my end.

I'm not in a rush but I would like to get this resolved before the week is out. Let me know what you need from me.

Thanks,
Michelle Kemp
Hartford, CT

Hi Michelle,

Thank you for following up and for that screenshot — it told us exactly what we needed to know. The issue was with a Bushnell Park route preset that's been defaulting to a shorter inner-loop baseline, which was capping your logged distance regardless of the actual path you were taking. I've manually corrected the distance profile on your account and updated the GPS anchor points so the full perimeter route is now recognized.

Your 2–2.5 mile morning loop should log accurately starting with your next session. I've also flagged the preset to our maps team since it's likely affecting other users on that same route and they'll want to push a fix on their end.

Give it a try tomorrow and reply back here if anything still looks off. We'll stay on it until it's right.

Best,
Customer Support Team

Hi Roberto,

Following up on my schedule adjustment request from last week — I still haven't seen anything come through from admin, and I need this sorted before the end of the month. My caseload right now is heavy, and I can't keep absorbing the overflow hours without a comp-time arrangement or a formal schedule change on record. It's not sustainable, and I'm not going to pretend it is.

I'm in the office Tuesday through Thursday most weeks. If there's a form I'm missing or a step in the process I skipped, just tell me and I'll handle it. I'd rather fix it on my end than have this sit another two weeks.

Thanks for your time.
Michelle Kemp
Senior Social Worker, Elm City Family Services

Hi Michelle,

Thank you for following up — I do see your request came in on the 19th, and I apologize for the delay on our end. I've flagged it with the scheduling coordinator directly, and you should receive a confirmation no later than end of day Thursday. Going forward, requests of this type route through the HR portal under "Schedule Modifications" — that queue tends to move faster than the general inbox.

Sorry again for the lag. Let me know if anything else comes up in the meantime.

Best,
Employee Support, Elm City Family Services

Roberto,

Thursday works — I'll keep an eye out. And good to know about the HR portal; I'll bookmark it so next time I'm not doing a whole search-and-rescue operation to find the right channel.

One thing while I have you: does the comp-time policy cover community outreach hours, or is it only direct client-facing time? I had a Saturday event last month that I logged, but I wasn't sure how it gets classified. My mornings are usually spoken for during the week so those Saturday hours add up.

Appreciate you getting this moving.
Michelle

Hi Roberto,

Following up on the ticket I submitted last Thursday — the lunchtime reminder notifications for my daily call with my mom are still not coming through. I call her every weekday around noon, and that 11:45 alert was the whole reason I signed up for this service. I missed the ping twice last week and had to hear about it both times, which, trust me, is its own kind of consequence.

I'm on an iPhone 14, notifications enabled, app fully updated. Already did the uninstall-reinstall per the last agent's suggestion. Still nothing.

Can someone look at the back end on this rather than walking me through the same steps again? This is a daily routine — it's not optional for me.

Thanks,
Michelle Kemp

Hi Michelle,

Thank you for following up and I'm sorry for the ongoing frustration — that's a legitimate disruption to a routine that clearly matters. I pulled your account and found the issue: a June 22nd system update reset custom notification windows for a subset of accounts back to the 9:00 AM default. Yours was in that group.

I've manually restored your 11:45 AM reminder on our end — no action needed from you. If you'd like to confirm it's reflected, check Settings > Reminders > Custom. Allow up to 24 hours for it to populate fully.

Again, I apologize you had to follow up twice to get here. That shouldn't have happened.

Best,
Customer Support | ConnectWell

Roberto,

Okay, that actually tracks — I noticed something felt off after an update but couldn't isolate what. Checked settings like you said and the 11:45 slot is back. Got the alert right on time this morning, so I'd call it fixed.

One small thing while I have you: the notification now reads "Scheduled Call" instead of the custom label I'd set. Minor, I know, but I set "Mama — lunch call" deliberately so it reads different from a work reminder at a glance — when I'm mid-session with a client, that split-second matters. Is that something I can restore myself or does it need your end?

Appreciate the quicker turnaround once it got escalated.

— Michelle

Michelle,

Glad the timing is back where it belongs! The custom label is fully self-serve — Settings > Reminders > Custom > tap your reminder > Edit Label. Type it right back in and it saves immediately. That field wasn't part of the June 22nd update scope, so no risk of it getting wiped again.

Also noted on the escalation comment — I'm flagging the original ticket internally so the team can review why the account-level reset wasn't caught in the first round of troubleshooting. You shouldn't have had to come back a second time.

Hope the noon calls stay on schedule from here. Take care, Michelle.

Customer Support | ConnectWell

Hi Roberto,

Following up on my ticket from last week — still haven't gotten a response and I'm a little surprised. My wind-down routine is genuinely one of the things keeping me together on tough days. I do case management work, so by the time I get home the tank is near empty. The journal app is the last thing I touch before I blow out the candle and cut the lights, and it's been locking up mid-entry three nights running now.

Losing a full entry after a day like mine is not a small thing to me, even if it sounds like one on paper. Can someone please look at the sync issue on my account?

Thanks,
Michelle Kemp

Hi Michelle,

I sincerely apologize for the delay — that's not the experience we aim to provide, and I especially hear you on how much that routine matters after the kind of days you're describing. We pulled up your account and identified the issue: a sync conflict between local draft saves and cloud backup that's been triggering timeouts on longer entries.

We've pushed a fix directly to your account. Force-close the app once, reopen it, and you should be good. Auto-save will now kick in every 30 seconds going forward so nothing gets lost mid-entry again.

If anything acts up tonight, reply here and I'll escalate it personally — no more waiting. Thank you for your patience, and genuinely, thank you for the work you do.

Customer Support

Hi Roberto,

Following up on the ticket I submitted last week regarding Gmail API access for our case management platform. I still haven't received the OAuth credentials I requested, and at this point it's holding up a workflow integration we're trying to get running for our intake team here at Elm City Family Services.

To recap: I need a service account with Gmail API scope enabled so our system can send automated follow-up emails to families on our waitlist. The IT contact I was working with before referred me to your team, and I've been bouncing between departments ever since. Not ideal.

Can you confirm whether the ticket (ref #GML-40872) is still open and who currently owns it? I'd like a resolution or at least a clear timeline by end of week if at all possible.

Thanks,
Michelle Kemp
Senior Social Worker, Elm City Family Services
Hartford, CT

Hi Michelle,

Apologies for the runaround — that's not the experience we want you to have, and I appreciate you following up directly. I pulled ticket #GML-40872 and I can confirm it's still open on our end. It got flagged for a secondary review because the requested scope includes gmail.send, which requires a quick domain verification step on our side before we can issue credentials.

The good news: domain verification for elmcityfamilyservices.org came back clean this morning. I'm provisioning the service account now and will have the JSON key file and setup documentation over to you within the hour. I'll also CC your IT contact so everyone's on the same page going forward.

Let me know if you run into anything during setup — happy to jump on a quick call if it's easier.

Best,
Roberto
Google Workspace Support

Roberto,

Got the credentials — thank you. Setup went smoothly once I had the JSON key; your documentation was actually clear, which, no offense, is not always the case with these things.

We ran a test send this afternoon and the integration is working. Families on the waitlist will start getting automated touchpoints starting Monday, which is honestly a big deal for our intake team. We've been doing that manually, and between caseloads, things fall through cracks they shouldn't.

Really appreciate you moving on it quickly once you picked it up. Closing this one out on my end.

Take care,
Michelle Kemp
Elm City Family Services

Hi Roberto,

Following up on ticket #88412 from last week — the Google Calendar API integration we're trying to get running for our intake scheduling at Elm City Family Services. I still haven't received the updated OAuth 2.0 credentials your team said would be re-issued after the project ID got reset. My IT contact here confirmed the service account was recreated on our end, so the hold-up is on your side.

I'm not trying to be difficult — I just have back-to-back client blocks most days and I can only troubleshoot this in the morning before my caseload kicks in. Right now we're still manually entering appointments, which is nobody's favorite thing.

Can you confirm where things stand and give me an actual ETA? I'd appreciate it.

Thanks,
Michelle Kemp
Elm City Family Services, Hartford

Hi Michelle,

Thank you for following up, and I apologize for the delay. I can confirm the OAuth 2.0 credentials were re-issued yesterday evening — they should now be available in your Google Cloud Console under IAM & Admin > Service Accounts. The relevant service account is listed as elm-city-intake-svc@elmcity-intake-prod.iam.gserviceaccount.com.

You'll want to download the new JSON key file and replace the old one in your application config. Also make sure the Calendar API is still enabled under APIs & Services — occasionally a project reset will toggle that off.

Let me know once you've had a chance to test. I'm keeping this ticket open until you confirm the sync is working.

Best,
Customer Support
Google Workspace Support

Roberto,

Okay, progress — I found the service account and pulled the new JSON key this morning before my walk. Dropped it into the config, restarted the service, and the API calls are going through now without the 403. Small victory.

One thing I'm still seeing: events created in our internal system are syncing to the Google Calendar, but updates to existing events aren't pushing through. Create works, patch doesn't. I checked the scopes and we have https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar so it shouldn't be a permissions issue. Could be something in how we're passing the eventId on the PATCH request but wanted to flag it with you before I go further down that road.

I'll test a few more things tomorrow morning and report back. But we're closer than we were, so I'll take it.

Michelle

Hi Michelle,

Glad the auth issue is resolved! On the PATCH problem — you're right to look at the eventId. The most common cause is that the eventId being passed is URL-encoded when it shouldn't be, or vice versa. Try logging the exact eventId value your system pulls from the initial create response and confirm it's being passed as a plain string in the PATCH URI, not re-encoded.

Also worth checking: if your events are created with a specific calendar ID (not 'primary'), make sure the PATCH request is targeting that same calendarId. A mismatch there will silently fail or 404 depending on your error handling.

I'm attaching a minimal working PATCH example to this email for reference. If it's still not pushing through after those checks, reply here and we can schedule a 20-minute screenshare — just flag a morning window that works for you.

Almost there. You've got this.

Best,
Customer Support
Google Workspace Support

Hi Roberto,

Following up on the ticket I opened last week regarding the Google Drive API integration for our client intake system here at Elm City Family Services. My IT contact said the OAuth credentials were approved on your end, but our case managers are still hitting a 403 when they try to pull shared drive documents through the app. Deadline-wise, this is holding up three staff members who handle the bulk of our Hartford caseload.

Can you confirm whether the service account was granted the right Drive scopes? We need drives.readonly and drive.file at minimum. If you need me to share the error logs, I can pull those this afternoon — just let me know the format you need them in.

Appreciate the quick turnaround on this one.

Michelle Kemp
Senior Social Worker, Elm City Family Services

Hi Michelle,

Thanks for the follow-up — sorry this stretched into another week on your end. I confirmed the issue: the service account was provisioned with drive.file scope only, which doesn't cover shared drives. I've pushed the updated scope bundle (drives.readonly + drive.file + drive.appdata) through our admin console and the change should propagate within the next 30 minutes.

Once it's live, have one of your case managers retry the document pull and let me know if the 403 clears. If it persists, grab the x-request-id header from the failed response and send it my way — that'll let me pull the exact server-side trace without needing the full logs.

We'll get your team back up and running before end of day.

Best,
API Support Specialist, Google Workspace

Hi Roberto,

Following up on my ticket from last week regarding the WhatsApp Business API integration for Elm City Family Services. We're trying to get our outreach line approved through Meta so case managers can send appointment reminders to clients — a lot of our families communicate primarily through WhatsApp, so this is a real barrier to access for us.

I submitted the business verification documents on June 19th and haven't received confirmation that they were accepted. Ticket number is #ELM-7724. Can you tell me where things stand in the review queue and whether anything else is needed on our end?

Appreciate the help.
Michelle Kemp
Senior Social Worker, Elm City Family Services | Hartford, CT

Hi Michelle,

Thanks for following up — I pulled up ticket #ELM-7724 and can confirm your documents came through on June 19th. They're currently in the business identity review queue, and turnaround is running 7–10 business days, so you should have an update by end of next week.

One thing worth flagging: the display name you submitted — "Elm City Family Services Outreach" — has been routed to secondary review because of the service descriptor at the end. That can tack on an extra 2–3 days. If you're open to simplifying it to just "Elm City Family Services," I can request the change before it advances further and likely save you some time.

Let me know how you'd like to handle it and I'll get it updated right away.

Best,
WhatsApp Business Support

Roberto,

Make the change — "Elm City Family Services" is fine. We need the line working; the display name is not the hill I want to die on.

One thing I should have caught earlier: the number we registered (+1-860-555-0193) is a Google Voice line we've been using for the program. I want to confirm now whether that's eligible for API access or if we need to port to a direct carrier number before approval comes through. Finding that out after the fact would set us back considerably, and I don't have that kind of runway right now.

Thanks for staying on it.
Michelle
Elm City Family Services | Hartford, CT

Hi Roberto,

Following up on my inquiry from last week about the Bushnell Park walking path conditions. I do a 2–3 mile loop most mornings before work and I'd noticed some of the north trail near the fountain had gotten pretty uneven — wanted to flag it formally since I wasn't sure if anyone from the parks office had been out there yet.

For context, I'm usually out there around 6:30 a.m., dark roast in hand, so I see that stretch in all conditions. The root uplift by the elm grove is the specific spot I'm most concerned about — real trip hazard, especially in low light.

Just wanted to make sure the right people had eyes on it. Let me know if you need more detail or photos.

Thanks,
Michelle Kemp

Hi Michelle,

Thank you for reaching out and for the detailed heads-up — this is exactly the kind of report that helps us stay on top of maintenance before someone gets hurt. I've logged the concern under ticket #BPK-2247 and forwarded it to our grounds crew supervisor, Denise, who covers the north trail section.

Denise mentioned she can get someone out there for an assessment by Wednesday morning. Given your schedule, would you be comfortable if a crew member contacted you directly if they have questions about the exact location? Totally optional — the ticket note is pretty thorough as-is.

Also appreciate the early-morning intel. Those 6 a.m. regulars are honestly our best eyes on the ground.

Best,
Parks & Recreation Support

Roberto,

Wednesday works great — I'll be out there as usual so if Denise's crew is around the elm grove between 6:15 and 7, they're welcome to flag me down. Happy to walk them right to the spot, saves everyone time.

And yes, they're welcome to reach out directly. My contact is on the original form but just in case: 860-555-0174.

One more thing while I have you — the bench near the west pond has a loose armrest, nothing urgent, but worth adding to the list whenever someone's next out that way. I practically live on that bench on Fridays when the week has been a lot. Don't need a ticket for it, just passing it along.

Thanks for moving on this quickly.

Michelle

Michelle,

Perfect — I've added a note to the ticket so Denise's crew knows to look for you in that window. They'll appreciate it; pinpointing root damage from a work order description alone is more art than science.

And I've added the west pond bench to our routine maintenance queue — no separate ticket needed, it'll get folded into Wednesday's run. Consider it handled.

We're glad to have folks like you keeping an eye on things. Bushnell's a special place and it takes a village to keep it that way. Hope the rest of your week is lighter than the one that sent you to that bench.

Take care,
Parks & Recreation Support | City of Hartford

Hi Roberto,

Following up on the work hours inquiry I submitted last week — I haven't received a confirmation yet and I want to make sure it didn't fall through the cracks. My standard schedule at Elm City Family Services runs Monday through Friday, roughly 8:30 to 5, though client emergencies don't always respect that. I'm flagging a discrepancy on my timesheet for the week of June 16th; I logged two after-hours home visits that don't appear to have been captured correctly.

If there's a form I need to resubmit or a supervisor signature required, just let me know and I'll get it handled. I'd like this resolved before the next pay period closes.

Thanks for your help,
Michelle Kemp
Senior Social Worker, Elm City Family Services

Hi Michelle,

Thank you for following up — you're right, it looks like your original request got stuck in the queue and didn't route properly. I apologize for the delay.

I've pulled up your timesheet for the week of June 16th and I can see the two entries in question. They were flagged for supervisor approval but it looks like that step wasn't completed on the back end. I've re-triggered the approval workflow and sent a prompt to your supervisor so this should move quickly. You'll get an automated confirmation once it clears.

For future after-hours visits, the fastest path is submitting through the HR portal same day with the visit type marked "field" — that routes it differently and skips a step. Happy to walk you through it if that would help.

Should be resolved well before the pay period closes. Let me know if anything else comes up.

Best,
HR Support | Elm City Family Services

Hi Roberto,

Following up on the weekday lunch call arrangement for my mother, Carmen Kemp. We set this up a few weeks ago and overall it's been working — but I noticed a missed connection last Thursday around 12:15. I'm usually on my own call with her around that window (it's basically the one reliable overlap I have during my workday at Elm City Family Services), so when I couldn't get through I did a quick check and she was fine. Just a timing mix-up on her end.

That said, I want to make sure that miss got logged. Is there a way to flag when a scheduled call doesn't connect and send a notification to me? I'm not trying to micromanage the process — I just like knowing the loop is closed. Even a quick text or email would do it.

Let me know what's possible.

Michelle Kemp

Hi Michelle,

Thank you for following up, and glad to hear Carmen was okay. I pulled Thursday's log — our system shows a no-answer at 12:13 PM with one retry at 12:43 PM, also unanswered. Since our escalation threshold is three consecutive misses, no alert was triggered. I completely understand why a single missed connection still matters to you.

The good news: we do have a single-miss notification option — it's just not enabled by default. I can turn that on for Carmen's file today and route it to either your email or phone, whichever is easier to catch mid-shift. I'll also flag the 12:00–12:30 window as a potential conflict so our team is aware.

If a later timeslot would work better around your own call with her, we can adjust the schedule too. Just let me know.

Client Support | Elm City Caregiver Connect

Roberto,

Email works best for the notification — I'm in and out of it between cases all day, so I'll catch it faster there than a call. Please go ahead and enable that.

And yes, shifting to 12:30 would probably solve most of it. The noon slot keeps colliding with when I'm already on the phone with her anyway, which is a little counterproductive.

I appreciate you moving on this quickly. Caseload has been heavy and the small logistics are exactly what starts slipping through the cracks first. One less thing to track in the back of my head genuinely helps.

Thank you,
Michelle

Hi,

I placed an order (#47821) about two weeks ago for the Sandalwood & Cedar jar candle — the one I keep at home specifically for winding down after hard days. What showed up was a citrus blend I never ordered. I work with families in crisis, and when I finally get to close the journal, light something, and tell myself the day is actually over, the scent matters. Citrus at 10 p.m. is not it.

I've been a subscriber for about eight months and this is the first mix-up, so I'm not writing to escalate — I just need the right candle and some direction on whether you need the wrong one back.

Thanks,
Michelle Kemp

Hi Michelle,

Thank you for writing in, and I'm sorry for the mix-up. I pulled up your order and can confirm the Sandalwood & Cedar was selected correctly at checkout — this was entirely on our end. I've already processed a replacement and you'll receive a shipping confirmation within 24 hours. No need to return the citrus.

Eight months as a subscriber means a lot to us, and I don't want your evening routine disrupted any longer than it already has been. I've added a complimentary wax melt sampler to your shipment as an apology — no strings attached.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if anything else comes up.

Best,
Customer Support | Hearth & Wick Co.

Roberto,

Appreciated the fast turnaround — confirmation came through this morning. One follow-up question before the package arrives: the sampler you added, is any of it in a warm or woody range? After a full day in the field I need something that says "you're done, go to sleep" — not something that wakes me back up. If it's all citrus and linen I'll probably just set it aside.

Also, small update: the citrus candle has been rehomed to my office desk. It's actually doing fine there during the 9 a.m. case reviews.

Thanks again,
Michelle

Hi Michelle,

Good news — three of the five melts in the sampler land squarely in the warm/woody family: Amber & Vetiver, Smoked Vanilla, and Black Cedar. The remaining two are lighter (linen and green tea), so you can pass those along guilt-free. Amber & Vetiver in particular runs quiet and heavy — based on what you described, I think that one will do exactly what you need it to do.

Glad the citrus found a purpose. Thank you for being patient with us, and for the note about your routine — it's a good reminder of why the details matter. Hope the next delivery is exactly right.

Take care,
Customer Support | Hearth & Wick Co.
